headline: Samsung and SK Hynix tumble 7%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: This is the most direct hit to your book today. The semiconductor selloff that started on Wall Street is now a global story, with the world's biggest memory chipmakers dropping hard. SMH, SOXL, and any chip-adjacent name you hold are all exposed to this sentiment contagion.
rec: Seriously consider trimming SOXL here before it tests $200 - on 3X leverage with margin behind it, a continued chip rout could be brutal. SMH stop at $540 is your canary; if it breaks, SOXL gets uglier fast.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom - hidden ways to trade it
detail: Robotics and AI hardware remain a long-term secular story, and ARM and ALAB (both in your scanner) are cited as adjacent beneficiaries. However, today's chip rout creates near-term headwinds even for quality names in this theme.
rec: ARM has a scanner ACCUMULATE setup (entry $336.80-337.13, stop $336.18) and strong daily/4H uptrend bias, but entering a chip-adjacent name on a day Samsung is down 7% is risky - watch this setup, don't chase it today.

headline: Europe can't quit Chinese air conditioners amid trade rebalancing push
detail: This highlights ongoing US-China-Europe trade tension, which is background noise for semiconductor supply chains. Chip stocks are already sensitive to tariff and export control headlines, so any escalation adds to the pressure your book is already feeling today.
rec: No specific trade here, but this reinforces why chip names stay volatile - keep stops tight and don't add leverage into this environment.

headline: Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals
detail: China's tech giants are aggressively moving into robotics and AI hardware, which intensifies competition for the same market Nvidia and its ecosystem are targeting. Long-term this adds competitive pressure, near-term it's more noise than signal for your specific holdings.
rec: Nothing actionable for your book today from this headline specifically - it's a slow-burn competitive dynamic, not a reason to buy or sell anything you hold right now.
